name: Razorpay String Intelligence Engine
scenario: Razorpay's fraud detection team needs string similarity metrics to detect suspicious merchant IDs. Build a string DP engine that computes LCS, edit distance, and anagram relationships between millions of string pairs daily.

core.description: lcs(String s1, String s2): O(m×n) time O(n) space (rolling array). Test: lcs('abcde','ace')=3, lcs('abc','def')=0.
editDistance(String s1, String s2): O(m×n) time O(n) space rolling array. Test: editDistance('horse','ros')=3, editDistance('','abc')=3.
longestPalindromeSubseq(String s): use LCS(s, reverse(s)). Test: lps('bbbab')=4, lps('cbbd')=2.
shortestCommonSupersequenceLen(String s1, String s2): m+n-lcs(s1,s2). Test: ('abac','cab')=5.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): lcs=3, editDistance=3, lps=4, scs=5. All pass.
README: explains rolling array optimization for edit distance — which cells dp[i][j] depends on and why two rows suffice.

bonus.description: wildcardMatch(String s, String p): O(m×n) DP with '?' and '*'. Test: ('adceb','*a*b')=true, ('aa','a')=false.
distinctSubsequences(String s, String t): O(m×n). Test: ('rabbbit','rabbit')=3.
minDeleteSumASCII(String s1, String s2): ASCII-weighted delete cost. Test: ('sea','eat')=231.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. LCS DP recurrence: if s1[i-1]==s2[j-1]: dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1 (extend). Else: dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]) — skip one char from either. EditDistance: match → dp[i-1][j-1] (free). Mismatch → 1+min(dp[i-1][j-1]=replace, dp[i-1][j]=delete, dp[i][j-1]=insert). Base cases: dp[i][0]=i, dp[0][j]=j. Rolling array: dp[i][j] only needs row i-1 and column j-1 of same row → keep prev[] and curr[]. Swap arrays after each outer iteration. LPS = LCS(s, reverse(s)): a palindromic subsequence must read same in both directions, which is exactly characters that appear in s AND reversed(s) in the same order.
